evals.prompt:
Implement a student enrollment feature for a university management system. Students can enroll in courses, but each course has a maximum capacity. If the course is full, enrollment should be rejected with a clear error. The system needs a POST /enrollments endpoint.

The project is at /tmp/uni-mgmt and uses Node.js with Express and TypeORM, following hexagonal architecture (src/[module]/application, src/[module]/domain, src/[module]/infrastructure). It already has a course creation feature at:
- src/courses/domain/Course.ts (entity)
- src/courses/domain/CourseId.ts (value object)
- src/courses/application/CourseCreator.ts (use case)
- src/courses/infrastructure/TypeOrmCourseRepository.ts (adapter)
- src/courses/infrastructure/CoursePostController.ts (route handler)
- test/courses/application/CourseCreator.test.ts (unit test)
- test/courses/domain/CourseMother.ts (object mother)

Use Jest for testing.
evals.expected_output: Coordinator spawns Explore agent for codebase discovery, classifies as backend-only, spawns backend-architect (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /backend-architect), uses EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode for plan approval, spawns backend-developer (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /backend-developer), spawns reviewer (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /reviewer, runs git diff internally), delegates report to agent
expectations: Spawns an Explore agent (subagent_type: 'Explore') in Phase 1 for codebase discovery — the orchestrator does NOT call codebase-retrieval or Glob directly
Classifies scope as backend-only — does not spawn frontend-architect or frontend-developer agents

Spawns a backend-architect agent (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /backend-architect) with a self-contained prompt that includes the task description, tech stack (Node.js/Express/TypeORM), and architecture (hexagonal). The prompt starts with 'Activate /backend-architect.' to invoke the skill

Calls EnterPlanMode after the backend-architect completes, writes a plan summary to the plan file covering scope, key decisions, files to create, test strategy, and agent order, then calls ExitPlanMode — does NOT spawn any developer agents before receiving user approval

Spawns a backend-developer agent (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /backend-developer) with a prompt that includes the complete architecture plan from the backend-architect and the project path. The prompt starts with 'Activate /backend-developer.' to invoke the skill

After the backend-architect completes, spawns a general-purpose agent (mode: 'bypassPermissions') to persist the plan to {project_path}/.agents/plans/student-enrollment-api-backend.md with YAML frontmatter (feature, scope, side, revision, created_at, updated_at)
After the backend-developer completes, spawns an Explore agent to validate implementation completeness against the architect plan — returns a checklist of DONE/PARTIAL/MISSING items

Spawns a reviewer agent (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /reviewer) after the backend-developer completes and validation passes, with a prompt that instructs the reviewer to run git diff internally. The prompt starts with 'Activate /reviewer.' to invoke the skill. The orchestrator does NOT run git diff or pass a diff to the reviewer
Delegates the final report to a spawned agent — the orchestrator does NOT compose the report directly. The report includes an 'Implementation Completeness' section

evals.prompt:
Build a product catalog feature for an e-commerce application. The backend needs a GET /products endpoint with filtering by category and price range, plus a POST /products endpoint for admin product creation. The frontend needs a product listing page with filter controls and a product card grid.

The project is at /tmp/ecommerce-app. The backend uses Node.js with Express and TypeORM, following hexagonal architecture (src/[module]/application, src/[module]/domain, src/[module]/infrastructure). The frontend uses Next.js 14 App Router (app/[route]/page.tsx) with Tailwind CSS.

The project already has:
- Backend: src/orders/domain/Order.ts, src/orders/application/OrderCreator.ts, src/orders/infrastructure/OrderPostController.ts
- Frontend: app/orders/page.tsx, app/orders/components/OrderCard.tsx
- Tests: test/orders/application/OrderCreator.test.ts, app/orders/__tests__/page.test.tsx

Use Jest for testing on both sides.
evals.expected_output: Coordinator spawns Explore agent, classifies as full-stack, spawns both architects (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /backend-architect and 'frontend-architect') in parallel, uses EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode for plan approval after both complete, spawns backend-dev (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /backend-developer) first then frontend-dev (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /frontend-developer) sequentially, reviewer (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /reviewer) runs git diff internally, delegates report to agent
expectations: Spawns an Explore agent (subagent_type: 'Explore') in Phase 1 for codebase discovery — the orchestrator does NOT call codebase-retrieval or Glob directly
Classifies scope as full-stack — recognizes that both API endpoints (GET/POST /products) and a frontend page (product listing) are needed

Spawns backend-architect (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /backend-architect) AND frontend-architect (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /frontend-architect) in parallel (same turn, two Agent tool calls) with self-contained prompts that include the task description and respective tech stacks. Each prompt starts with 'Activate /[skill-name].' to invoke the respective skill

Calls EnterPlanMode after BOTH architects complete, writes a combined plan summary to the plan file covering scope, key decisions from both plans, files to create for both backend and frontend, test strategy, and agent order, then calls ExitPlanMode — does NOT spawn any developer agents before receiving user approval

After BOTH architects complete, spawns a general-purpose agent (mode: 'bypassPermissions') to persist plans to {project_path}/.agents/plans/ — writes both a-product-catalog-feature-backend.md and a-product-catalog-feature-frontend.md with YAML frontmatter

Spawns backend-developer (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /backend-developer) FIRST, waits for completion, validates backend implementation via Explore agent, THEN spawns frontend-developer (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /frontend-developer) — sequential ordering because the frontend depends on the backend API contract
After the frontend-developer completes, spawns an Explore agent to validate frontend implementation completeness against the frontend architect plan

Spawns a single reviewer agent (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /reviewer) after BOTH developers complete and validation passes, with a prompt that instructs the reviewer to run git diff internally. The prompt starts with 'Activate /reviewer.' to invoke the skill. The orchestrator does NOT run git diff or pass a diff to the reviewer
Delegates the final report to a spawned agent covering the full-stack scope, including an 'Implementation Completeness' section

evals.prompt:
Refactor the dashboard page in our analytics application. The current dashboard component is a single 500-line file that handles data fetching, state management, chart rendering, and filter controls all in one component. Break it into smaller, focused components following single responsibility principle.

The project is at /tmp/analytics-app. It uses Next.js 14 with Pages Router (pages/dashboard.tsx) and Tailwind CSS. Charts use Recharts. State management uses React Context.

Existing structure:
- pages/dashboard.tsx (the monolith to refactor)
- components/Layout.tsx (app shell with sidebar)
- context/DashboardContext.tsx (state provider)
- hooks/useDashboardData.ts (data fetching hook)
- __tests__/dashboard.test.tsx (existing tests)

Use Jest with React Testing Library.

After the frontend-developer completes, spawns an Explore agent to validate implementation completeness against the architect plan — returns a checklist of DONE/PARTIAL/MISSING items

evals.prompt:
Implement a tag management feature for a content management system. Articles can have multiple tags, and tags can be created and listed. The system needs POST /tags and GET /tags endpoints, plus a PUT /articles/:id/tags endpoint to assign tags to an article. This is CSU-312.

The project is at /tmp/cms-api and uses Node.js with Express and TypeORM, following hexagonal architecture (src/[module]/application, src/[module]/domain, src/[module]/infrastructure). It already has:
- src/articles/domain/Article.ts (entity)
- src/articles/domain/ArticleId.ts (value object)
- src/articles/application/ArticleCreator.ts (use case)
- src/articles/infrastructure/TypeOrmArticleRepository.ts (adapter)
- src/articles/infrastructure/ArticlePostController.ts (route handler)
- test/articles/application/ArticleCreator.test.ts (unit test)

Use Jest for testing.
evals.expected_output: Coordinator spawns Explore agent, detects Linear issue code CSU-312 (used only for the autonomous-mode trigger check), runs the standard 6-phase pipeline (backend-architect → EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode → backend-developer → reviewer → report). The orchestrator never spawns a QA agent, never invokes the linear CLI to create a QA sub-issue, and the final report has no QA Sub-Issue section.
expectations: Spawns an Explore agent (subagent_type: 'Explore') in Phase 1 for codebase discovery — the orchestrator does NOT call codebase-retrieval or Glob directly

Detects 'CSU-312' as a Linear issue code in the task description during Phase 1 analysis — the code is recorded only to feed the autonomous-mode trigger check (fetching the upstream Linear issue for an ADR/PRD reference), not to drive any sub-issue creation

Runs the 6-phase pipeline: spawns backend-architect (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /backend-architect), uses EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode for plan approval, spawns backend-developer (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /backend-developer), spawns reviewer (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /reviewer, runs git diff internally), then composes the final Phase 6 report
Does NOT spawn a QA sub-issue agent or any agent dedicated to QA-ticket creation after the reviewer completes — Phase 6 is Report, not QA Sub-Issue

Does NOT invoke `linear issue create` (or any shell command that creates a Linear sub-issue with a `QA:` prefix) at any point in the pipeline — regression guard against the removed Phase 6 behavior per docs/adr/0006-remove-qa-sub-issue-from-do-task.md
Does NOT write a `/tmp/qa-plan-*.md` file or any other QA-plan artifact — the playbook no longer carries a QA Plan Template
The final Phase 6 report does NOT contain a `### QA Sub-Issue` section — the report template was updated when Phase 6 was removed

evals.prompt:
Refactor the PaymentProcessor class in our billing service. It's a 300-line god class with a giant switch statement handling 5 payment methods (credit_card, paypal, bank_transfer, crypto, gift_card). Each case has its own validation, processing, and notification logic inlined. There are no tests for this class. Extract each payment method into its own strategy class using the Strategy pattern.

The project is at /tmp/billing-service and uses Node.js with Express and TypeORM, following hexagonal architecture. Existing structure:
- src/payments/domain/PaymentProcessor.ts (the 300-line god class)
- src/payments/domain/Payment.ts (entity)
- src/payments/application/ProcessPayment.ts (use case that calls PaymentProcessor)
- src/payments/infrastructure/PaymentPostController.ts (route handler)
- test/payments/ (empty directory — no existing tests)

Use Jest for testing.
evals.expected_output: Coordinator spawns Explore agent, classifies as backend-only. Spawns backend-architect (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /backend-architect) whose plan includes a Tech Debt Impact section. The backend-developer (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /backend-developer) prompt includes ATDD enforcement. The reviewer (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /reviewer) runs git diff internally and verifies ATDD discipline.
expectations: Spawns an Explore agent (subagent_type: 'Explore') in Phase 1 for codebase discovery — the orchestrator does NOT call codebase-retrieval or Glob directly

Spawns a backend-architect (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /backend-architect) whose prompt includes the ATDD planning instructions — the architect should identify tech debt (god class, switch statement, no tests) and plan characterization tests before refactoring
The architecture plan presented via EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode includes a Tech Debt Impact section or equivalent identifying the existing tech debt and the ATDD approach

The backend-developer (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /backend-developer) prompt includes ATDD enforcement instructions — write characterization tests to lock current PaymentProcessor behavior before extracting strategy classes

The reviewer (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /reviewer) prompt instructs the reviewer to run git diff internally and includes ATDD verification instructions — verify characterization tests exist that lock previous behavior, verify acceptance tests exist for the new strategy pattern
The final report (composed by a report agent) reflects ATDD discipline: characterization tests written before refactoring, acceptance tests for the strategy pattern

evals.prompt:
Add a tags feature with a new tags table to a CMS API. Create a migration to add the tags table (id, name, slug, created_at) and a pivot table article_tags (article_id, tag_id). Then implement the Tag domain model and TagCreator use case.

The project is at /tmp/cms-api and uses Node.js with Express and TypeORM, following hexagonal architecture. Existing migrations:
- src/shared/infrastructure/migrations/20260301120000_create_articles.ts
- src/shared/infrastructure/migrations/20260302093000_add_article_status.ts

Use Jest for testing.
evals.expected_output: Coordinator spawns Explore agent, classifies as backend-only. The backend-developer (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /backend-developer) prompt includes the migration datetime rule requiring YYYYMMDDHHmmss format. The reviewer (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /reviewer) runs git diff internally and includes migration filename verification.
expectations: Spawns an Explore agent (subagent_type: 'Explore') in Phase 1 for codebase discovery — the orchestrator does NOT call codebase-retrieval or Glob directly

The backend-developer (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /backend-developer) prompt includes the database migration rule: migration filenames must use `YYYYMMDDHHmmss` format (full datetime with seconds)

The reviewer (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /reviewer) prompt instructs the reviewer to run git diff internally and includes the instruction to verify database migration filenames use `YYYYMMDDHHmmss` format and flag non-compliant filenames as Major
The architecture plan references migration files with datetime-with-seconds format (e.g., 20260303XXXXXX_create_tags.ts where XXXXXX includes hours, minutes, AND seconds)

The overall pipeline still follows the standard flow: Explore agent → backend-architect (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /backend-architect) → EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode → backend-developer (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /backend-developer) → reviewer (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /reviewer, internal git diff) → report agent

The system needs a PUT /users/:id/notification-preferences endpoint and a GET /users/:id/notification-preferences endpoint. This is CSU-500.

- src/users/domain/User.ts (entity)
- src/users/domain/UserId.ts (value object)
- src/users/application/UserFinder.ts (use case)
- src/users/infrastructure/TypeOrmUserRepository.ts (adapter)

- test/users/application/UserFinder.test.ts (unit test)
- test/users/domain/UserMother.ts (object mother)

Use Jest for testing.
evals.expected_output: Coordinator completes all 6 phases without stopping after Phase 4. After implementation and validation (Phase 4), proceeds to review (Phase 5), then directly to the final report (Phase 6). CSU-500 is detected in Phase 1 but only used to fetch the upstream Linear issue for the autonomous-mode trigger check — no QA sub-issue is ever created. The orchestrator never presents an implementation summary or declares 'done' before Phase 6.
expectations: After Phase 4 validation completes, spawns a reviewer agent (subagent_type: 'general-purpose', prompt activates /reviewer, Phase 5) that runs git diff internally — does NOT present a summary or declare 'done'
After Phase 5 review completes, proceeds directly to Phase 6 (Report) — does NOT spawn a QA sub-issue agent or any QA-ticket-creation agent, even though Linear issue code CSU-500 was detected

Does NOT invoke `linear issue create` (or any shell command that creates a Linear sub-issue with a `QA:` prefix) at any point in the pipeline — regression guard against the removed Phase 6 behavior per docs/adr/0006-remove-qa-sub-issue-from-do-task.md
CSU-500 is recorded in Phase 1 only to feed the autonomous-mode trigger check (fetching the upstream issue for an ADR/PRD reference), not to drive sub-issue creation
All 6 phases execute in order — the pipeline does not terminate before the Phase 6 report is composed and presented

evals.expected_output: Coordinator runs the full 6-phase pipeline. The Phase 6 report includes a 'Human QA Checklist' section with concrete, observable steps for verifying enrollment success and capacity rejection. The checklist includes Setup, Happy Path, Error Cases, Edge Cases, and Regression subsections with API call examples.
expectations: The Phase 6 report includes a '### Human QA Checklist' section
The QA checklist includes a 'Happy Path' subsection with a concrete step for successful enrollment (e.g., POST /enrollments with valid data → expect 201)
The QA checklist includes an 'Error Cases' subsection with a concrete step for capacity rejection (e.g., POST /enrollments for a full course → expect error response)
The QA checklist includes 'Setup', 'Edge Cases', and 'Regression' subsections
Steps are concrete and observable — they specify HTTP methods, endpoints, expected status codes, and response shapes (not vague descriptions)
The report includes a re-entry instruction for fixing QA findings via /do-task
